diagrams.description: This timeline traces the evolution of Organizational Psychology. Key milestones: Taylor's Scientific Management (1911) and Münsterberg's 'Psychology and Industrial Efficiency' (1913) established the field. The Hawthorne studies (1924‑1932) launched the human relations movement. Mid‑century saw Maslow's hierarchy of needs (1943), the formation of APA Division 14 (1945, later SIOP), Herzberg's two‑factor theory (1959), and Vroom's expectancy theory (1964). The job characteristics model (1976) transformed job design. The 1990s emphasized stress, well‑being, and work‑life balance. The 2000s brought positive organizational behavior (2002) and people analytics (2010s). Contemporary themes include remote work, DEI, and digital transformation (2020s).
